meta.source: ONS Labour demand volumes by SOC 2020 (online job adverts, Textkernel); pay: live ASHE Table 15 medians for 363 occupations (region x 4-digit SOC); profiles from National Careers Service
meta.licence: Open Government Licence v3.0 — source: Office for National Statistics

fields.name: Admin and Secretarial
fields.about: Keeping organisations running — scheduling, records, correspondence and coordination.
fields.routes: Open to entry-level starters with good organisation and IT skills; business-administration apprenticeships are a common formal route.
fields.note: A broad first rung that touches every department, in slow long-term decline as tasks automate.

roles.name: Other administrative occupations
roles.desc: Keeps an office running with scheduling, records, correspondence and data.

roles.name: Records clerks and assistants
roles.desc: An administrative role keeping an organisation organised and running.

roles.name: Receptionists
roles.desc: The first point of contact for visitors and callers, handling enquiries and bookings.

roles.name: Financial administrative occupations
roles.desc: Keeps an office running with scheduling, records, correspondence and data.

roles.name: Residential, day and domiciliary care managers and proprietors
roles.desc: An administrative role keeping an organisation organised and running.

roles.name: Office supervisors
roles.desc: An administrative role keeping an organisation organised and running.

roles.name: Managers in storage and warehousing
roles.desc: An administrative role keeping an organisation organised and running.

roles.name: Property, housing and estate managers
roles.desc: An administrative role keeping an organisation organised and running.

fields.name: Teaching and Education
fields.about: Helping people learn — from supporting children in classrooms to teaching subjects and training adults.
fields.routes: Teaching-assistant roles need few formal qualifications; qualified teaching needs a degree plus PGCE or a salaried teaching apprenticeship.
fields.note: Strongly seasonal, peaking before the September term.

roles.name: Teaching assistants
roles.desc: Supports teachers in the classroom and helps individual pupils with their learning.

roles.name: Other vocational and industrial trainers
roles.desc: Trains people in practical, job-specific skills in workplaces or colleges.

roles.name: Secondary education teaching professionals
roles.desc: Teaches a specialist subject to older pupils and prepares them for exams.

roles.name: Educational support assistants
roles.desc: An education role helping people learn and develop.

roles.name: Early education and childcare practitioners
roles.desc: An education role helping people learn and develop.

roles.name: Primary education teaching professionals
roles.desc: Plans and teaches across the curriculum to children in primary school.

roles.name: Special and additional needs education teaching professionals
roles.desc: An education role helping people learn and develop.

roles.name: Teachers of English as a foreign language
roles.desc: Plans and delivers lessons, assessing progress and supporting pupils' learning.

fields.name: Finance, Accounting and Insurance
fields.about: Recording, analysing and managing money and risk for organisations — including insurance.
fields.routes: Start as an accounts assistant and study AAT then ACCA or CIMA while working; underwriting and actuarial work have their own qualification routes.
fields.note: Stable and structured, with clear qualification ladders.

roles.name: Book-keepers, payroll managers and wages clerks
roles.desc: Keeps financial records accurate and makes sure people and bills are paid correctly.

roles.name: Business and financial project management professionals
roles.desc: A finance, accounting or insurance role managing money and risk.

roles.name: Finance and investment analysts and advisers
roles.desc: A finance, accounting or insurance role managing money and risk.

roles.name: Business associate professionals
roles.desc: A finance, accounting or insurance role managing money and risk.

roles.name: Health and safety managers and officers
roles.desc: A finance, accounting or insurance role managing money and risk.

roles.name: Financial accounts managers
roles.desc: Records and reports on an organisation's finances, from bookkeeping to management accounts.

roles.name: Buyers and procurement officers
roles.desc: A finance, accounting or insurance role managing money and risk.

roles.name: Taxation experts
roles.desc: Advises on tax, prepares returns and helps clients stay compliant and efficient.

fields.name: Hospitality, Leisure and Personal Care
fields.about: Preparing and serving food and drink, running venues, and personal-care and leisure services — social, flexible-hours work.
fields.routes: Most roles offer immediate starts with on-the-job training; chefs, hairdressers and beauticians often train through apprenticeships or college courses.
fields.note: Crashed hardest in 2020, then rebounded sharply as venues reopened.

roles.name: Kitchen and catering assistants
roles.desc: A hospitality, leisure or personal-care role serving and looking after customers.

roles.name: Chefs
roles.desc: Prepares and cooks food to order in a professional kitchen, managing quality and timing.

roles.name: Waiters and waitresses
roles.desc: Greets and serves customers, taking orders and looking after their experience.

roles.name: Bar staff
roles.desc: Serves drinks, handles payments and keeps the bar running and stocked.

roles.name: Catering and bar managers
roles.desc: A hospitality, leisure or personal-care role serving and looking after customers.

roles.name: Cleaning and housekeeping managers and supervisors
roles.desc: A hospitality, leisure or personal-care role serving and looking after customers.

roles.name: Sports coaches, instructors and officials
roles.desc: Leads exercise or coaching sessions and helps people train safely towards their goals.

roles.name: Housekeepers and related occupations
roles.desc: A hospitality, leisure or personal-care role serving and looking after customers.

fields.name: Skilled Trades & Maintenance
fields.about: Keeping buildings, grounds and equipment working, clean and secure — practical, steady work needed everywhere.
fields.routes: Cleaning, caretaking and security roles offer easy entry; technical and repair roles build on trade skills and short certifications.
fields.note: Practical, steady work needed everywhere.

roles.name: Cleaners and domestics
roles.desc: Keeps premises clean, hygienic and presentable.

roles.name: Metal working production and maintenance fitters
roles.desc: Operates machinery and assembles products on a production line.

roles.name: Security guards and related occupations
roles.desc: Protects people, premises and property, monitoring and deterring risks.

roles.name: Plumbers and heating and ventilating installers and repairers
roles.desc: Installs and repairs water, heating and drainage systems.

roles.name: Metal machining setters and setter-operators
roles.desc: A maintenance or facilities role keeping buildings and equipment working and secure.

roles.name: Launderers, dry cleaners and pressers
roles.desc: Keeps premises clean, hygienic and presentable.

roles.name: Electrical and electronic trades
roles.desc: A maintenance or facilities role keeping buildings and equipment working and secure.

roles.name: Skilled metal, electrical and electronic trades supervisors
roles.desc: A maintenance or facilities role keeping buildings and equipment working and secure.

fields.name: Health and Nursing
fields.about: Hands-on work caring for patients and supporting clinical teams in hospitals, GP surgeries, care settings and the community.
fields.routes: Enter as a healthcare assistant or support worker with no formal qualifications and train on the job; nursing needs a degree, increasingly available as a paid apprenticeship.
fields.note: Among the largest and most resilient fields.

roles.name: Other registered nursing professionals
roles.desc: Plans and delivers clinical care to patients, monitoring conditions and giving treatment.

roles.name: Specialist medical practitioners
roles.desc: A healthcare role caring for patients and supporting clinical teams.

roles.name: Welfare and housing associate professionals
roles.desc: A healthcare role caring for patients and supporting clinical teams.

roles.name: Other health professionals
roles.desc: A healthcare role caring for patients and supporting clinical teams.

roles.name: Youth and community workers
roles.desc: A healthcare role caring for patients and supporting clinical teams.

roles.name: Veterinarians
roles.desc: A healthcare role caring for patients and supporting clinical teams.

roles.name: Registered nurse practitioners
roles.desc: Plans and delivers clinical care to patients, monitoring conditions and giving treatment.

roles.name: Pharmacists
roles.desc: A healthcare role caring for patients and supporting clinical teams.

fields.name: Transport and Logistics
fields.about: Moving and storing goods — driving, picking and packing, and coordinating deliveries.
fields.routes: Warehouse roles start immediately with no qualifications; HGV driving needs a licence, often employer-funded.
fields.note: Surged through the pandemic as online retail boomed, especially driving roles.

roles.name: Warehouse operatives
roles.desc: Picks, packs and moves stock in a warehouse, keeping orders flowing.

roles.name: Heavy and large goods vehicle drivers
roles.desc: A transport or logistics role moving and storing goods.

roles.name: Delivery drivers and couriers
roles.desc: Delivers parcels and goods to homes and businesses on a set route.

roles.name: Mobile machine drivers and operatives
roles.desc: A transport or logistics role moving and storing goods.

roles.name: Transport and distribution clerks and assistants
roles.desc: A transport or logistics role moving and storing goods.

roles.name: Road transport drivers
roles.desc: A transport or logistics role moving and storing goods.

roles.name: Fork-lift truck drivers
roles.desc: A transport or logistics role moving and storing goods.

roles.name: Managers in transport and distribution
roles.desc: A transport or logistics role moving and storing goods.

fields.name: Social Care
fields.about: Supporting people who need help with daily life — older people, those with disabilities or illness — in their homes and in care settings.
fields.routes: Very open entry valuing reliability and empathy; the Care Certificate and NVQs are gained on the job. Social work requires a degree.
fields.note: Deep, growing demand and very open entry.

roles.name: Care workers and home carers
roles.desc: Supports older or disabled people with everyday tasks like washing, dressing, meals and medication.

roles.name: Nursing auxiliaries and assistants
roles.desc: Plans and delivers clinical care to patients, monitoring conditions and giving treatment.

roles.name: Early education and childcare assistants
roles.desc: Supports older or disabled people with everyday tasks like washing, dressing, meals and medication.

roles.name: Senior care workers
roles.desc: Supports older or disabled people with everyday tasks like washing, dressing, meals and medication.

roles.name: Social workers
roles.desc: Assesses and safeguards vulnerable children or adults and arranges the support they need.

roles.name: Houseparents and residential wardens
roles.desc: A social-care role supporting people who need help with daily life.

roles.name: Protective service associate professionals
roles.desc: A social-care role supporting people who need help with daily life.

roles.name: Animal care services occupations
roles.desc: A social-care role supporting people who need help with daily life.

fields.name: Construction and Trades
fields.about: Skilled hands-on building and installation work on sites and in homes — practical, physical and varied.
fields.routes: Apprenticeships are the classic route; many also start labouring and gain trade qualifications (NVQs, CSCS card) while working.
fields.note: Skilled trades are in chronic short supply, so qualified tradespeople have real bargaining power.

roles.name: Vehicle technicians, mechanics and electricians
roles.desc: Installs, tests and maintains electrical wiring and equipment in buildings.

roles.name: Electricians and electrical fitters
roles.desc: Installs, tests and maintains electrical wiring and equipment in buildings.

roles.name: Production managers and directors in construction
roles.desc: Operates machinery and assembles products on a production line.

roles.name: Quantity surveyors
roles.desc: A construction or skilled-trade role doing hands-on building and installation work.

roles.name: Estimators, valuers and assessors
roles.desc: Works out the likely cost or value of building work, materials or property.

roles.name: Elementary construction occupations
roles.desc: A construction or skilled-trade role doing hands-on building and installation work.

roles.name: Construction operatives
roles.desc: A construction or skilled-trade role doing hands-on building and installation work.

roles.name: Carpenters and joiners
roles.desc: Makes and fits wooden structures and fittings, from frames and floors to staircases.

fields.name: Sales
fields.about: Persuading customers and businesses to buy, and managing those relationships — target-driven and sociable.
fields.routes: Most roles hire on attitude and communication rather than qualifications; you typically start as an executive and progress on performance.
fields.note: Hires constantly and rewards results over credentials.

roles.name: Sales accounts and business development managers
roles.desc: Records and reports on an organisation's finances, from bookkeeping to management accounts.

roles.name: Business sales executives
roles.desc: Wins and manages customer relationships, working to sales targets.

roles.name: Marketing, sales and advertising directors
roles.desc: Wins and manages customer relationships, working to sales targets.

roles.name: Sales administrators
roles.desc: Keeps an office running with scheduling, records, correspondence and data.

roles.name: Estate agents and auctioneers
roles.desc: Helps people buy, sell and rent property, valuing homes and arranging viewings.

roles.name: Sales related occupations
roles.desc: Wins and manages customer relationships, working to sales targets.

roles.name: Vehicle and parts salespersons and advisers
roles.desc: Wins and manages customer relationships, working to sales targets.

roles.name: Telephone salespersons
roles.desc: Wins and manages customer relationships, working to sales targets.

fields.name: IT and Technology
fields.about: Building, running and supporting the software and systems organisations rely on — much of it remote or hybrid.
fields.routes: Helpdesk roles take entry-level starters; bootcamps, certifications and degree apprenticeships get people in without a computer-science degree.
fields.note: Highly remote-friendly, with a sharp 2021-22 hiring surge that has since cooled.

roles.name: Programmers and software development professionals
roles.desc: Designs, writes and tests the code behind applications, websites and systems.

roles.name: IT user support technicians
roles.desc: First port of call for technical problems, fixing hardware, software and access issues for users.

roles.name: IT operations technicians
roles.desc: A technology role building, running or supporting software and systems.

roles.name: Cyber security professionals
roles.desc: Protects systems and data from attack, monitoring threats and responding to incidents.

roles.name: Information technology professionals
roles.desc: A technology role building, running or supporting software and systems.

roles.name: IT project managers
roles.desc: A technology role building, running or supporting software and systems.

roles.name: Information technology directors
roles.desc: A technology role building, running or supporting software and systems.

roles.name: Information technology trainers
roles.desc: Trains people in practical, job-specific skills in workplaces or colleges.

fields.name: Manufacturing
fields.about: Making and assembling products on production lines and operating machinery — often shift-based.
fields.routes: Operative roles start immediately with on-the-job training; routes into supervision and skilled maintenance via NVQs and apprenticeships.
fields.note: Strong in the Midlands and North, with shift premiums.

roles.name: Production managers and directors in manufacturing
roles.desc: Operates machinery and assembles products on a production line.

roles.name: Food, drink and tobacco process operatives
roles.desc: A manufacturing role making, assembling or checking products.

roles.name: Metal making and treating process operatives
roles.desc: A manufacturing role making, assembling or checking products.

roles.name: Routine inspectors and testers
roles.desc: A manufacturing role making, assembling or checking products.

roles.name: Mining and quarry workers and related operatives
roles.desc: A manufacturing role making, assembling or checking products.

roles.name: Textile process operatives
roles.desc: A manufacturing role making, assembling or checking products.

roles.name: Quality assurance technicians
roles.desc: Sets and checks standards so products and services consistently meet requirements.

roles.name: Other skilled trades
roles.desc: A manufacturing role making, assembling or checking products.

fields.name: Consulting
fields.about: Advising organisations on how to work better, stay compliant and manage change — analytical, client-facing work.
fields.routes: Most consultants come from a relevant degree or industry experience; graduate schemes and analyst roles are common entry points.
fields.note: London-heavy and steadily growing across regulation and transformation work.

roles.name: IT business analysts, architects and systems designers
roles.desc: Bridges business needs and solutions, mapping processes and defining requirements.

roles.name: Financial managers and directors
roles.desc: A consulting role advising organisations on how to work better.

roles.name: Management consultants and business analysts
roles.desc: Advises organisations on how to improve performance, strategy and operations.

roles.name: Quality assurance and regulatory professionals
roles.desc: Guides organisations through laws and regulations so products and practices stay compliant.

roles.name: Functional managers and directors
roles.desc: A consulting role advising organisations on how to work better.

roles.name: Purchasing managers and directors
roles.desc: A consulting role advising organisations on how to work better.

roles.name: Health services and public health managers and directors
roles.desc: A consulting role advising organisations on how to work better.

roles.name: Chief executives and senior officials
roles.desc: A consulting role advising organisations on how to work better.

fields.name: Engineering
fields.about: Designing, building and maintaining physical things and systems — machines, buildings, vehicles, power.
fields.routes: Engineering apprenticeships (level 3 up to degree level) are a strong debt-free route; technician roles open up via BTEC and HNC qualifications.
fields.note: Spread across the country and tied to manufacturing and infrastructure.

roles.name: Engineering professionals
roles.desc: Designs, builds or maintains technical systems and equipment.

roles.name: Engineering technicians
roles.desc: Designs, builds or maintains technical systems and equipment.

roles.name: Mechanical engineers
roles.desc: Designs, develops and maintains mechanical systems, machines and products.

roles.name: Civil engineers
roles.desc: Plans and supervises construction of infrastructure such as roads and bridges.

roles.name: Science, engineering and production technicians
roles.desc: Operates machinery and assembles products on a production line.

roles.name: Engineering project managers and project engineers
roles.desc: Designs, builds or maintains technical systems and equipment.

roles.name: Electrical engineers
roles.desc: Designs and oversees electrical systems and equipment.

roles.name: Production and process engineers
roles.desc: Operates machinery and assembles products on a production line.

fields.name: Science and Research
fields.about: Investigating, testing and developing through lab and field research — methodical, evidence-driven work.
fields.routes: Laboratory-technician roles suit science graduates and apprentices; research roles typically build on a relevant degree, sometimes postgraduate.
fields.note: Clustered around research hubs like Cambridge, Oxford and the North West.

roles.name: IT managers
roles.desc: A science or research role investigating, testing and developing.

roles.name: Research and development (RandD) managers
roles.desc: Designs and runs experiments to develop new knowledge or products.

roles.name: CAD, drawing and architectural technicians
roles.desc: A science or research role investigating, testing and developing.

roles.name: Environment professionals
roles.desc: A science or research role investigating, testing and developing.

roles.name: IT network professionals
roles.desc: A science or research role investigating, testing and developing.

roles.name: Laboratory technicians
roles.desc: Prepares, runs and records lab tests and keeps equipment and samples in order.

roles.name: Social and humanities scientists
roles.desc: A science or research role investigating, testing and developing.

roles.name: Biochemists and biomedical scientists
roles.desc: Studies substances and their reactions to develop or test materials and products.

fields.name: Retail
fields.about: Selling to and serving customers in shops, plus stock, displays and store running.
fields.routes: Sales-assistant roles need no qualifications and offer immediate starts; progression into supervisor and store manager is performance-based.
fields.note: Highly accessible and everywhere, with lots of part-time hours.

roles.name: Sales and retail assistants
roles.desc: Wins and manages customer relationships, working to sales targets.

roles.name: Pharmacy and optical dispensing assistants
roles.desc: A retail role selling to and serving customers in store.

roles.name: Managers and directors in retail and wholesale
roles.desc: Serves customers in store, handles payments and keeps the shop running.

roles.name: Sales supervisors - retail and wholesale
roles.desc: Wins and manages customer relationships, working to sales targets.

roles.name: Debt, rent and other cash collectors
roles.desc: A retail role selling to and serving customers in store.

roles.name: Shopkeepers and owners - retail and wholesale
roles.desc: Serves customers in store, handles payments and keeps the shop running.

roles.name: Shelf fillers
roles.desc: A retail role selling to and serving customers in store.

roles.name: Retail cashiers and check-out operators
roles.desc: Serves customers in store, handles payments and keeps the shop running.

fields.name: HR and Recruitment
fields.about: Finding, supporting and developing an organisation's people — recruiting, advising on policy, handling pay and training.
fields.routes: Recruitment hires on drive and people skills with no set qualifications; HR roles build via CIPD qualifications, often started as an administrator.
fields.note: A well-trodden way into a professional office career.

roles.name: Human resources and industrial relations officers
roles.desc: Supports an organisation's people — pay, policy, hiring and wellbeing.

roles.name: Human resource managers and directors
roles.desc: Supports an organisation's people — pay, policy, hiring and wellbeing.

roles.name: Human resources administrative occupations
roles.desc: Keeps an office running with scheduling, records, correspondence and data.

fields.name: Customer Service
fields.about: Helping customers by phone, chat and email — answering questions, solving problems and handling complaints.
fields.routes: Hired on communication skills rather than qualifications, with full training provided; a strong stepping stone into sales or operations.
fields.note: A reliable, increasingly home-based entry route.

roles.name: Customer service occupations
roles.desc: Helps customers by phone, chat and email, answering questions and solving problems.

roles.name: Customer service managers
roles.desc: Helps customers by phone, chat and email, answering questions and solving problems.

roles.name: Call and contact centre occupations
roles.desc: Helps customers by phone, chat and email, answering questions and solving problems.

roles.name: Customer service supervisors
roles.desc: Helps customers by phone, chat and email, answering questions and solving problems.

roles.name: Communication operators
roles.desc: A customer-service role helping customers and resolving issues.

roles.name: Telephonists
roles.desc: A customer-service role helping customers and resolving issues.

fields.name: Marketing and PR
fields.about: Promoting products and brands across digital channels, content, events and PR.
fields.routes: Junior digital and content roles are reachable via short courses, platform certifications and a portfolio; marketing apprenticeships exist too.
fields.note: Creative and fast-moving, with lots of junior digital roles.

roles.name: Advertising and marketing associate professionals
roles.desc: Promotes products and brands across channels, content and campaigns.

roles.name: Marketing and commercial managers
roles.desc: Promotes products and brands across channels, content and campaigns.

roles.name: Public relations professionals
roles.desc: A marketing or PR role promoting products, brands and content.

roles.name: Advertising accounts managers and creative directors
roles.desc: Records and reports on an organisation's finances, from bookkeeping to management accounts.

roles.name: Public relations and communications directors
roles.desc: A marketing or PR role promoting products, brands and content.

fields.name: Legal
fields.about: Advising on and handling the law — drafting documents, managing cases and ensuring compliance.
fields.routes: Paralegal and legal-secretary roles are accessible entry points; qualifying as a solicitor now has a degree route and a paid apprenticeship.
fields.note: Concentrated in London, but paralegal roles are realistic entry points.

roles.name: Solicitors and lawyers
roles.desc: Advises clients on the law and represents them in legal matters.

roles.name: Legal professionals
roles.desc: A legal role advising on and handling the law.

roles.name: Legal associate professionals
roles.desc: A legal role advising on and handling the law.

roles.name: Legal secretaries
roles.desc: A legal role advising on and handling the law.

roles.name: Barristers and judges
roles.desc: A legal role advising on and handling the law.

fields.name: Creative and Media
fields.about: Designing and producing visual and digital content — graphics, interfaces, interiors, video and imagery.
fields.routes: Portfolio over paper: self-taught skills, short courses and bootcamps open doors, especially in UX/UI and interior design.
fields.note: Portfolio matters more than formal qualifications.

roles.name: Dancers and choreographers
roles.desc: A creative or media role designing and producing content.

roles.name: Arts officers, producers and directors
roles.desc: A creative or media role designing and producing content.

roles.name: Graphic and multimedia designers
roles.desc: A creative or media role designing and producing content.

roles.name: Authors, writers and translators
roles.desc: A creative or media role designing and producing content.

roles.name: Photographers, audio-visual and broadcasting equipment operators
roles.desc: Takes and edits images for clients, publications, products or events.

roles.name: Design occupations
roles.desc: A creative or media role designing and producing content.

roles.name: Interior designers
roles.desc: Plans and designs indoor spaces, balancing function, style and budget.

roles.name: Managers and directors in the creative industries
roles.desc: A creative or media role designing and producing content.

fields.name: Data and Analytics
fields.about: Finding patterns in data and turning them into decisions — from dashboards to forecasting and research.
fields.routes: Junior analyst roles are reachable through short courses, certifications and a portfolio of projects; many come from numerate degrees.
fields.note: One of the fastest-growing fields, spread across every industry.

roles.name: Actuaries, economists and statisticians
roles.desc: Designs studies and applies statistical methods to draw reliable conclusions from data.

roles.name: Data analysts
roles.desc: Turns raw data into clear insights and dashboards that help an organisation make decisions.

roles.name: Market research interviewers
roles.desc: Gathers and interprets data on customers and markets to guide strategy and marketing.

fields.name: Energy and Utilities
fields.about: Generating and distributing power and running utilities — increasingly renewable.
fields.routes: Engineering and technician apprenticeships are widely funded by employers; trade and electrical backgrounds transfer well into renewables.
fields.note: Fast-growing as renewables expand, strong in Scotland and the North East.

roles.name: Water and sewerage plant operatives
roles.desc: Helps generate, distribute or maintain power, water or other utilities.

roles.name: Energy plant operatives
roles.desc: Helps generate, distribute or maintain power, water or other utilities.

roles.name: Production managers and directors in mining and energy
roles.desc: Operates machinery and assembles products on a production line.
